person.name: Rumi
person.description: Jalāl al-Dīn Muḥammad Rūmī,[b] commonly known as Rumi (30 September 1207 – 17 December 1273), was a Sufi mystic, poet, and founder of the Islamic brotherhood known as the Mevlevi Order. His family hailed from Balkh. Rumi is an influential figure in Sufism, and his thought and works loom large both in Persian literature and mystic poetry in general. Today, his translated works are enjoyed all over the world.

datapoints.description: Rumi's family moved to Samarkand, Uzbekistan, after leaving Wakhsh due to the advancing Mongol hordes. He spent his formative years in this city.

sources.title: Gemini 2.5 Flash Lite extraction from Wikipedia: Rumi

datapoints.description: Rumi and his family may have encountered the poet Attar in Nishapur, Iran, during their westward flight from the Mongols. Attar is said to have given Rumi a book.

sources.title: Gemini 2.5 Flash Lite extraction from Wikipedia: Rumi

datapoints.description: After leaving Nishapur, Rumi's family passed through Baghdad, Iraq, and met with scholars and Sufis.

sources.title: Gemini 2.5 Flash Lite extraction from Wikipedia: Rumi

datapoints.description: The caravan Rumi was part of journeyed to Hejaz, Saudi Arabia, where they performed the pilgrimage at Mecca.

sources.title: Gemini 2.5 Flash Lite extraction from Wikipedia: Rumi

datapoints.description: Rumi performed the Islamic pilgrimage at Mecca, Saudi Arabia, as part of his family's westward journey.

sources.title: Gemini 2.5 Flash Lite extraction from Wikipedia: Rumi

datapoints.description: After Hejaz, Rumi's caravan passed through Damascus, Syria. He is also said to have spent four years here at a later date.

sources.title: Gemini 2.5 Flash Lite extraction from Wikipedia: Rumi

datapoints.description: Rumi's migrating caravan passed through Malatya, Turkey, on their journey westward.

sources.title: Gemini 2.5 Flash Lite extraction from Wikipedia: Rumi

datapoints.description: Rumi's migrating caravan passed through Erzincan, Turkey, on their journey westward.

sources.title: Gemini 2.5 Flash Lite extraction from Wikipedia: Rumi

datapoints.description: Rumi's migrating caravan passed through Sivas, Turkey, on their journey westward.

sources.title: Gemini 2.5 Flash Lite extraction from Wikipedia: Rumi

datapoints.description: Rumi's migrating caravan passed through Kayseri, Turkey, on their journey westward.

sources.title: Gemini 2.5 Flash Lite extraction from Wikipedia: Rumi

datapoints.description: Rumi's migrating caravan passed through Nigde, Turkey, on their journey westward.

sources.title: Gemini 2.5 Flash Lite extraction from Wikipedia: Rumi

datapoints.description: Rumi's family settled in Karaman, Turkey, for seven years. During this time, Rumi's mother and brother died, and he married and had children.

sources.title: Gemini 2.5 Flash Lite extraction from Wikipedia: Rumi

datapoints.description: Rumi settled in Konya, Turkey, with his father in 1228. He lived, worked as a scholar and teacher, and eventually died and was buried in Konya.

sources.title: Gemini 2.5 Flash Lite extraction from Wikipedia: Rumi

datapoints.date_display: During his time in Konya, before 1244
datapoints.description: Rumi is said to have travelled to Damascus, Syria, and spent four years there during his time as a teacher and jurist.

sources.title: Gemini 2.5 Flash Lite extraction from Wikipedia: Rumi
